You are adversarially verifying ONE load-bearing correctness claim from the current Horizon T14 session (project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge), plus the faithfulness of 5 small new lemmas. Do NOT edit files (you are read-only). Report your verdict back to me in your final message, and file a concise `horizon inbox` note only if you find a genuine defect.

## Claim to verify (the important one): the Serre-twist sign convention

File `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SerreTwist.lean` defines `ProjTwist.serreTwist n m` on `Proj ℤ[X_i : i ∈ n]` by gluing trivial line bundles `O_{D₊(X_i)}` over the standard cover with transition isomorphisms. Key definitions to read:
- `awayFraction n i j` (~line 187): claimed to be `X_i / X_j` in the degree-0 localization `(ℤ[X]_{X_i X_j})₀`. Verify it really is X_i/X_j (numerator X_i·X_i, denominator (X_i X_j)^1).
- `twistTransition n m i j` (~line 329): `pullbackUnitIso (f i j) ≪≫ unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit i j)^m) ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso (t i j ≫ f j i)).symm` — i.e. multiplication by `(X_i/X_j)^m`, going from the pullback of the chart-`i` structure sheaf to the pullback of the chart-`j` structure sheaf, in `Scheme.Modules.glue`.

THE QUESTION: does this gluing datum define `O(m)` or `O(-m)`? Derive it INDEPENDENTLY from scratch (do not just re-confirm the session's reasoning). Standard approach: identify the local frame on `D₊(X_i)` implied by the trivialization, express it in the `j`-frame, and compare to the transition `(X_i/X_j)^m`; then compute the global sections `Γ(Proj, serreTwist n m)` as compatible families and check whether they are degree-`m` homogeneous polynomials (⟹ O(m), O(1) very ample) or zero for m>0 (⟹ O(-m)). State clearly: O(m) or O(-m), with the frame identity and the global-sections computation. The session claims O(m) (frame X_i^m, Γ(O(1)) = linear forms). Confirm or refute.

## Also sanity-check (faithfulness / non-vacuity, quick) — 5 new lemmas in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/ProjectiveMorphism.lean`:
- `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.locallyOfFiniteType`, `.isSeparated`, `.universallyClosed` — each derives the named property from `.isProper`. Check the statements are the intended ones (not vacuous / not mis-stated).
- `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.of_iso` — transfer of `IsProjectiveWith π L` to `L'` along `e : L ≅ L'`. Check direction is right.
- `ProjectiveSpace.isProjectiveWith_over (d S)` — `(ℙ(Fin (d+1); S) ↘ S).IsProjectiveWith (twistingSheaf (Fin (d+1)) S 1)` via the identity closed immersion. Check it is genuinely non-vacuous (identity is a closed immersion; the O(1) comparison is pullback-along-identity).

All 5 are already machine-verified axiom-clean, so focus on whether the STATEMENTS are faithful, not the proofs.

Return: a crisp verdict on the sign (O(m) vs O(-m)) with your independent derivation, and a one-line OK/NOT-OK for each of the 5 lemma statements.

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.ProjectiveSpace
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GlueDescent
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianQuot
10	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjSubstrate
11	
12	/-!
13	# The Serre twisting sheaf `O(m)` on the integral model of projective space
14	
15	Mathlib v4.31 has no twisted structure sheaf on `Proj`.  This file constructs
16	`O(m)` on the integral model `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]` of projective space as a
17	`SheafOfModules`, by gluing the trivial line bundles on the basic opens
18	`D₊(Xᵢ)` along the transition units `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, through the project descent
19	engine `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue` (`Picard/GlueDescent.lean`) —
20	the same pattern as the Grassmannian universal quotient
21	(`Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean`), in rank one.
22	
23	Main definitions:
24	
25	* `ProjTwist.basicOpenCover n` — the open cover of `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ]` by the
26	  `D₊(Xᵢ)`;
27	* `ProjTwist.awayUnit n i j` — the unit `Xᵢ/Xⱼ` of the degree-zero localized
28	  ring `(ℤ[Xᵢ]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`, and `ProjTwist.overlapUnit n i j` its image in
29	  `Γ(V(i,j), O)` on the scheme-theoretic overlap of the cover;
30	* `ProjTwist.twistTransition n m i j` — the rank-one transition isomorphism
31	  multiplication by `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, conjugated by the structure-sheaf pullback
32	  comparison `Modules.pullbackUnitIso`;
33	* `ProjTwist.serreTwist n m` — `O(m)` on `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`, the glued
34	  sheaf of modules transported along the cover isomorphism `fromGlued`.
35	
36	The convention is that the transition *from* the `i`-th trivialisation *to*
37	the `j`-th multiplies by `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, corresponding to the local generator
38	`Xᵢ^m` of `O(m)` on `D₊(Xᵢ)`.
39	
40	Blueprint: `def:serre_twist`, `lem:serre_twist_transition_unit`,
41	`lem:serre_twist_cocycle` (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
42	-/
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial HomogeneousLocalization
45	open AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian (scalarEnd scalarEnd_one scalarEnd_comp)
46	
47	noncomputable section
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
50	
51	universe u
52	
53	/-! ## Rank-one pullback coherences
54	
55	The Serre-twist cocycle transports rank-one transition isomorphisms of the shape
56	`pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).symm` to a triple
57	overlap.  The next block is the rank-one mirror of the free-sheaf coherence stack
58	of `GrassmannianQuot.lean` / `GlueDescent.lean` (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`,
59	`matrixEnd_pullback`, the `pullbackCongr` endpoint absorptions): with
60	`SheafOfModules.unit` in place of `SheafOfModules.free (Fin d)`, `scalarEnd` in
61	place of `matrixEnd`, and `pullbackUnitIso` in place of `pullbackFreeIso`.  Each
62	is immediate from an already-proven unit-level atom. -/
63	
64	namespace Scheme.Modules
65	
66	open AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian (scalarEnd scalarEnd_pullback)
67	
68	/-- Rank-one `map_comp` coherence (analogue of `pullbackFreeIso_comp`): the
69	structure-sheaf pullback comparison at a composite `b ≫ a` factors through the
70	pseudofunctor composition `pullbackComp`.  Immediate from the unit coherence
71	`gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (`(pullbackUnitIso f).hom = pullbackObjUnitToUnit f`). -/
72	lemma pullbackUnitIso_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) :
73	    (pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
74	        (pullbackUnitIso (b ≫ a)).hom
75	      = (pullback b).map (pullbackUnitIso a).hom ≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).hom :=
76	  gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp a b
77	
78	/-- Iso-conjugated form of the scalar-endomorphism pullback atom `scalarEnd_pullback`:
79	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) = Q.hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) ≫ Q.inv`, with
80	`Q = pullbackUnitIso p`.  The rank-one analogue of `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
81	lemma scalarEnd_pullback_iso {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
82	    (pullback p).map (scalarEnd a)
83	      = (pullbackUnitIso p).hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) ≫ (pullbackUnitIso p).inv := by
84	  have key : (pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ (pullbackUnitIso p).hom
85	      = (pullbackUnitIso p).hom ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a) :=
86	    scalarEnd_pullback p a
87	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← key, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
88	
89	/-- Closed zig-zag: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = 𝟙` for equal base
90	morphisms `φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr_hom`. -/
91	@[reassoc]
92	lemma pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr_hom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
93	    (pullbackUnitIso φ).inv ≫
94	        ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
95	        (pullbackUnitIso ψ).hom
96	      = 𝟙 _ := by
97	  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]
98	
99	/-- Left absorption: `pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = Q_φ` for equal base morphisms
100	`φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackCongr_hom_app_free`. -/
101	@[reassoc]
102	lemma pullbackCongr_hom_app_unit {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
103	    ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
104	        (pullbackUnitIso ψ).hom
105	      = (pullbackUnitIso φ).hom := by
106	  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]
107	
108	/-- Right absorption: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app = Q_ψ⁻¹` for equal base
109	morphisms `φ = ψ`.  Rank-one mirror of `pullbackFreeIso_inv_congr`. -/
110	@[reassoc]
111	lemma pullbackUnitIso_inv_congr {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
112	    (pullbackUnitIso φ).inv ≫
113	        ((pullbackCongr h).app (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).hom
114	      = (pullbackUnitIso ψ).inv := by
115	  subst h; simp [pullbackCongr]
116	
117	end Scheme.Modules
118	
119	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
120	/-- `topIso` naturality under `homOfLE` / structure-sheaf restriction: on a scheme `X`,
121	restriction of a global section from `V` to `U ⊆ V` commutes with the identifications
122	`Γ(U, ⊤) ≅ Γ(X, U)`. -/
123	lemma topIso_inv_homOfLE_appTop {X : Scheme.{u}} {U V : X.Opens} (e : U ≤ V) :
124	    V.topIso.inv ≫ (X.homOfLE e).appTop
125	      = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE e).op ≫ U.topIso.inv := by
126	  simp only [Scheme.Opens.topIso_inv, Scheme.homOfLE_appTop, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]
127	  exact congrArg _ (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
128	
129	namespace ProjTwist
130	
131	variable (n : Type u)
132	
133	/-- The variable `Xᵢ` is homogeneous of degree `1`. -/
134	lemma X_mem_deg_one (i : n) :
135	    (X i : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) ∈ homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 1 :=
136	  isHomogeneous_X _ _
137	
138	/-- The product `XᵢXⱼ` is homogeneous of degree `2`. -/
139	lemma X_mul_X_mem_deg_two (i j : n) :
140	    (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) ∈
141	      homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 2 :=
142	  SetLike.mul_mem_graded (X_mem_deg_one n i) (X_mem_deg_one n j)
143	
144	/-- The basic opens `D₊(Xᵢ)` cover `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ : i ∈ n]`: the variables
145	generate the coordinate ring over its degree-zero part. -/
146	lemma iSup_basicOpen_X_eq_top :
147	    ⨆ i, Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i) = ⊤ := by
148	  refine Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top' _ _ (fun i => ⟨1, X_mem_deg_one n i⟩) ?_
149	  rw [eq_top_iff]
150	  rintro a -
151	  have ha : a ∈ Algebra.adjoin (ULift.{u} ℤ)
152	      (Set.range (X : n → MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) := by
153	    rw [MvPolynomial.adjoin_range_X]; trivial
154	  induction ha using Algebra.adjoin_induction with
155	  | mem x hx => exact Algebra.subset_adjoin hx
156	  | algebraMap r =>
157	      have hr : (algebraMap (ULift.{u} ℤ) (MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) r
158	          ∈ homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0 := by
159	        rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
160	        exact isHomogeneous_C _ _
161	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem
162	        (Algebra.adjoin (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
163	          (Set.range (X : n → MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
164	        (⟨_, hr⟩ : homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ) 0)
165	  | add x y _ _ hx hy => exact add_mem hx hy
166	  | mul x y _ _ hx hy => exact mul_mem hx hy
167	
168	/-- The open cover of the integral model by the basic opens `D₊(Xᵢ)`. -/
169	def basicOpenCover :
170	    (Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))).OpenCover where
171	  I₀ := n
172	  X i := (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i)).toScheme
173	  f i := (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i)).ι
174	  mem₀ := by
175	    rw [Scheme.presieve₀_mem_precoverage_iff]
176	    refine ⟨fun x => ?_, inferInstance⟩
177	    have : x ∈ ⨆ i, Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i) := by
178	      rw [iSup_basicOpen_X_eq_top]; trivial
179	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp this
180	    exact ⟨i, ⟨x, hi⟩, rfl⟩
181	
182	/-- The glue datum of the basic-open cover of the integral model. -/
183	@[reducible] def glueData : Scheme.GlueData.{u} := (basicOpenCover n).gluedCover
184	
185	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xⱼ = Xᵢ²/(XᵢXⱼ)` as an element of the degree-zero
186	localized ring `(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`. -/
187	def awayFraction (i j : n) :
188	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) :=
189	  Away.mk _ (X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i j) 1 (X i * X i)
190	    (by simpa using X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i i)
191	
192	/-- The fraction `Xⱼ/Xᵢ = Xⱼ²/(XᵢXⱼ)` in the *same* localized ring
193	`(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀` — the inverse of `awayFraction`. -/
194	def awayFractionInv (i j : n) :
195	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) :=
196	  Away.mk _ (X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n i j) 1 (X j * X j)
197	    (by simpa using X_mul_X_mem_deg_two n j j)
198	
199	lemma awayFraction_mul_inv (i j : n) :
200	    awayFraction n i j * awayFractionInv n i j = 1 := by
201	  apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
202	  rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_mul, HomogeneousLocalization.val_one,
203	    awayFraction, awayFractionInv, Away.val_mk, Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_mul]
204	  have hnum : (X i * X i) * (X j * X j)
205	      = ((X i * X j) ^ 1 * (X i * X j) ^ 1 : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) := by
206	    ring
207	  rw [hnum]
208	  exact Localization.mk_self
209	    ((⟨(X i * X j) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
210	        Submonoid.powers (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) *
211	      (⟨(X i * X j) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
212	        Submonoid.powers (X i * X j : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ))))
213	
214	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xⱼ` as a unit of `(ℤ[X]_{XᵢXⱼ})₀`, with inverse
215	`Xⱼ/Xᵢ`. -/
216	def awayUnit (i j : n) :
217	    (Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j))ˣ where
218	  val := awayFraction n i j
219	  inv := awayFractionInv n i j
220	  val_inv := awayFraction_mul_inv n i j
221	  inv_val := (mul_comm _ _).trans (awayFraction_mul_inv n i j)
222	
223	/-! ## The transition units on the scheme-theoretic overlaps -/
224	
225	/-- The scheme-theoretic overlap of the glue datum factors through the basic
226	open `D₊(XᵢXⱼ) = D₊(Xᵢ) ⊓ D₊(Xⱼ)`. -/
227	lemma range_overlap_le (i j : n) :
228	    Set.range (pullback.fst ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ≫
229	        (basicOpenCover n).f i).base ⊆
230	      Set.range ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
231	        (X i * X j)).ι).base := by
232	  rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι, Proj.basicOpen_mul]
233	  rintro _ ⟨v, rfl⟩
234	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
235	  · have hx : ((pullback.fst _ _ ≫ (basicOpenCover n).f i) v : Proj _) ∈
236	        Set.range ⇑((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
237	          (X i)).ι) := by
238	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
239	      exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
240	    exact (Scheme.Opens.range_ι _) ▸ hx
241	  · have hcond := congrArg (fun (φ : pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i)
242	        ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ⟶ Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) =>
243	          φ.base v) pullback.condition
244	    simp only at hcond
245	    rw [hcond]
246	    have hx : ((pullback.snd _ _ ≫ (basicOpenCover n).f j) v : Proj _) ∈
247	        Set.range ⇑((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
248	          (X j)).ι) := by
249	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
250	      exact ⟨_, rfl⟩
251	    exact (Scheme.Opens.range_ι _) ▸ hx
252	
253	/-- The canonical morphism from the scheme-theoretic overlap of the `i`-th and
254	`j`-th charts to the basic open `D₊(XᵢXⱼ)`. -/
255	def overlapHom (i j : n) :
256	    pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ⟶
257	      (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).toScheme :=
258	  IsOpenImmersion.lift
259	    ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).ι)
260	    (pullback.fst ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j) ≫
261	      (basicOpenCover n).f i)
262	    (range_overlap_le n i j)
263	
264	/-- The ring homomorphism carrying degree-zero fractions over `XᵢXⱼ` to
265	sections of the structure sheaf on the scheme-theoretic overlap. -/
266	def overlapRingHom (i j : n) :
267	    Away (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j) →+*
268	      Γ(pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j), ⊤) :=
269	  ((overlapHom n i j).appTop.hom.comp
270	    ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))
271	      (X i * X j)).topIso.inv.hom)).comp
272	    (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).hom
273	
274	/-- The transition unit `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)` as a unit of the sections of the structure
275	sheaf on the scheme-theoretic overlap of the `i`-th and `j`-th charts. -/
276	def overlapUnit (i j : n) :
277	    Γ(pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f j), ⊤)ˣ :=
278	  Units.map (overlapRingHom n i j).toMonoidHom (awayUnit n i j)
279	
280	/-! ## Rank-one transitions -/
281	
282	/-- A unit of the global sections induces an automorphism of the structure
283	sheaf, multiplication by the unit (rank-one `matrixToFreeIso`). -/
284	def unitScalarIso {Y : Scheme.{u}} (v : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) :
285	    SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf where
286	  hom := scalarEnd v.val
287	  inv := scalarEnd v.inv
288	  hom_inv_id := by rw [scalarEnd_comp, v.val_inv, scalarEnd_one]
289	  inv_hom_id := by rw [scalarEnd_comp, v.inv_val, scalarEnd_one]
290	
291	@[simp]
292	lemma unitScalarIso_hom {Y : Scheme.{u}} (v : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) :
293	    (unitScalarIso v).hom = scalarEnd v.val := rfl
294	
295	@[simp]
296	lemma unitScalarIso_one {Y : Scheme.{u}} :
297	    unitScalarIso (1 : Γ(Y, ⊤)ˣ) = Iso.refl _ :=
298	  Iso.ext (by rw [Iso.refl_hom]; exact (congrArg scalarEnd Units.val_one).trans scalarEnd_one)
299	
300	/-- Iso-level structure-sheaf-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms,
301	`pullbackUnitIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport
302	(the rank-one analogue of `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso`). -/
303	lemma pullbackUnitIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) :
304	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso φ ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ψ).symm
305	      = eqToIso (congrArg
306	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj
307	            (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)) h) := by
308	  subst h; simp
309	
310	/-- The fraction `Xᵢ/Xᵢ` is `1`. -/
311	lemma awayFraction_self (i : n) : awayFraction n i i = 1 := by
312	  apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
313	  rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_one, awayFraction, Away.val_mk]
314	  have hden : (⟨(X i * X i) ^ 1, 1, rfl⟩ :
315	      Submonoid.powers (X i * X i : MvPolynomial n (ULift.{u} ℤ)))
316	      = ⟨X i * X i, 1, pow_one _⟩ := Subtype.ext (pow_one _)
317	  rw [hden]
318	  exact Localization.mk_self_mk _ _
319	
320	lemma awayUnit_self (i : n) : awayUnit n i i = 1 :=
321	  Units.ext (awayFraction_self n i)
322	
323	lemma overlapUnit_self (i : n) : overlapUnit n i i = 1 := by
324	  rw [overlapUnit, awayUnit_self, map_one]
325	
326	/-- The rank-one transition isomorphism of the Serre twist `O(m)`:
327	multiplication by `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m`, conjugated by the structure-sheaf pullback
328	comparisons on the scheme-theoretic overlap. -/
329	def twistTransition (m : ℕ) (i j : n) :
330	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((glueData n).f i j)).obj
331	        (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf) ≅
332	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).obj
333	        (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U j).ringCatSheaf) :=
334	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i j) ≪≫
335	    unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i j) ^ m) ≪≫
336	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).symm

338	/-- **(C1)** The diagonal transition of the Serre twist is the canonical
339	cast: `Xᵢ/Xᵢ = 1`, so the scalar automorphism is the identity and the two
340	pullback comparisons cancel. -/
341	lemma twistTransition_self (m : ℕ) (i : n) :
342	    twistTransition n m i i
343	      = eqToIso (congrArg
344	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
345	            (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf))
346	          (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
347	            rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])) := by
348	  have hmid : unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i i) ^ m)
349	      = Iso.refl (SheafOfModules.unit
350	          (pullback ((basicOpenCover n).f i) ((basicOpenCover n).f i)).ringCatSheaf) := by
351	    rw [overlapUnit_self, one_pow, unitScalarIso_one]
352	  calc twistTransition n m i i
353	      = Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫
354	          unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i i) ^ m) ≪≫
355	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
356	            ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm := rfl
357	    _ = Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫
358	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
359	            ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm := by
360	        refine (congrArg (fun e =>
361	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫ e ≪≫
362	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
363	              ((glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i)).symm) hmid).trans ?_
364	        exact congrArg (fun e => Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i i) ≪≫ e)
365	          (Iso.refl_trans _)
366	    _ = eqToIso (congrArg
367	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
368	            (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf))
369	          (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
370	            rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])) :=
371	      pullbackUnitIso_trans_symm_eqToIso
372	        (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
373	          rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])

375	/-- **The `m = 0` transition carries no twist.** The scalar automorphism

377	structure-sheaf comparison
378	`pullbackUnitIso fᵢⱼ ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso (tᵢⱼ ≫ fⱼᵢ)).symm` — exactly the
379	descent datum of the structure sheaf `𝒪 = O(0)`.  This is the all-indices
380	companion of `twistTransition_self` (which handles only the diagonal `i = j`)
381	and the algebraic core of `O(0) ≅ 𝒪`, validating the O(1) sign convention:
382	`m = 0` contributes no `Xᵢ/Xⱼ` factor. -/

384	    twistTransition n 0 i j

386	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).symm := by
387	  have hmid : unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i j) ^ 0) = Iso.refl _ := by
388	    rw [pow_zero, unitScalarIso_one]
389	  calc twistTransition n 0 i j
390	      = Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i j) ≪≫
391	          unitScalarIso ((overlapUnit n i j) ^ 0) ≪≫
392	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
393	            ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).symm := rfl
394	    _ = Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i j) ≪≫
395	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
396	            ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).symm := by
397	        refine (congrArg (fun e =>
398	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i j) ≪≫ e ≪≫
399	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso
400	              ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i)).symm) hmid).trans ?_
401	        exact congrArg (fun e =>
402	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso ((glueData n).f i j) ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl_trans _)

404	/-- **Transport of a rank-one scalar automorphism through
405	`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`** — the reusable abstract core of the Serre-twist
406	cocycle, the rank-one analogue of `pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso`
407	(`GrassmannianQuot.lean`).  A transition isomorphism of the twist shape
408	`pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫ (pullbackUnitIso b).symm` (multiplication
409	by a unit `v` of `Γ(V, ⊤)`, conjugated to the overlap pullbacks) transports along
410	`p : W ⟶ V` to the same shape over `p ≫ a` / `p ≫ b`, the scalar base-changed by
411	the comorphism `p.appTop`.  Combines the scalar-naturality atom
412	`scalarEnd_pullback_iso` with the pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackUnitIso_comp`. -/
413	lemma pullbackBaseChangeTransport_unitScalarIso {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V)
414	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{0}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj) (v : Γ(V, ⊤)ˣ) :
415	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b
416	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso a ≪≫ unitScalarIso v ≪≫
417	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b).symm)).hom
418	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ a)).hom ≫
419	        scalarEnd (p.appTop v.val) ≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)).inv := by
421	  simp only [Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom,
422	    Iso.symm_hom, unitScalarIso_hom]
423	  -- Front coherence: the `pullbackComp` cast + the `a`-leg comparison assemble into the
424	  -- composite comparison `Q_{p≫a}` (pseudofunctoriality, `pullbackUnitIso_comp`).
425	  have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
426	          (SheafOfModules.unit Yi.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
427	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso a).hom ≫
428	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p).hom
429	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ a)).hom := by
430	    erw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_comp a p]
431	    simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
432	    rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc]
433	  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into
434	  -- the inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`.
435	  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p).inv ≫
436	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b).inv ≫
437	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.unit Yj.ringCatSheaf)).hom
438	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)).inv := by
439	    have hiso : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.unit Yj.ringCatSheaf) ≪≫
440	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso (p ≫ b)
441	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso b) ≪≫
442	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso p := by
443	      apply Iso.ext
444	      simpa using Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso_comp b p
445	    have hinv := congrArg Iso.inv hiso
446	    simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv] at hinv
447	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Iso.app_hom]
448	    erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
449	    rw [Category.comp_id]
450	  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated scalar automorphism and apply the atom.
451	  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, Scheme.Modules.scalarEnd_pullback_iso]
452	  rw [← hfront, ← hback]
453	  rfl

455	/-! ## The Serre twisting sheaf
456	
457	The descent engine `Scheme.Modules.glue` (`Picard/GlueDescent.lean`) is
458	universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}` (see the NOTE at
459	`GlueDescent.lean:934`), so the glued sheaf itself is only available for a
460	`Type 0` index — which covers the intended consumers `n = Fin (m + 1)`
461	(`ℙ^m`).  The cover, units and transitions above stay universe-polymorphic
462	for a future generalisation of the engine. -/

464	/-! ## Substrate for the transported overlap cocycle
465	
466	The base cocycle `overlapUnit_cocycle_transport` (below) factors all three transition
467	units through a common map `V(i,j,k) ⟶ D₊(XᵢXⱼXₖ)` and reduces to the fraction identity
468	`(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = Xᵢ/Xₖ` in `A⁰_{XᵢXⱼXₖ}`.  The pieces below build that common map
469	(`commonHom`), the three chart-factorisations (`fact_ij/ik/jk`), the section-restriction
470	transport (`section_restrict`) and the fraction identity (`awayMap_awayFraction_cocycle`). -/

472	/-- The away-map fraction cocycle in `Away 𝒜 (XᵢXⱼXₖ)`:
473	`(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = Xᵢ/Xₖ` after mapping the three transition fractions into the common
474	degree-zero localised ring over `XᵢXⱼXₖ`. -/
475	lemma awayMap_awayFraction_cocycle (i j k : n) :
476	    awayMap (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X_mem_deg_one n k)
477	        (show X i * X j * X k = (X i * X j) * X k from rfl) (awayFraction n i j) *
478	      awayMap (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X_mem_deg_one n i)
479	        (show X i * X j * X k = (X j * X k) * X i from by rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm])
480	        (awayFraction n j k)
481	      = awayMap (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X_mem_deg_one n j)
482	        (show X i * X j * X k = (X i * X k) * X j from
483	          by rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm (X j) (X k), ← mul_assoc]) (awayFraction n i k) := by
484	  rw [awayFraction, awayFraction, awayFraction, awayMap_mk, awayMap_mk, awayMap_mk]
485	  apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
486	  simp only [HomogeneousLocalization.val_mul, Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_mul]
487	  rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff, Localization.r_iff_exists]
488	  refine ⟨1, ?_⟩
489	  simp only [OneMemClass.coe_one, one_mul, Submonoid.coe_mul]

642	          (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) xf a))
643	      = (Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) xt).topIso.inv
644	          (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) xt
645	            (awayMap (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) hg hx a)) := by
646	  have hnat := congrArg (fun (φ : _ ⟶ _) =>
647	      (CommRingCat.Hom.hom φ) (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) xf a))
648	    (topIso_inv_homOfLE_appTop (X := Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ))) le)
649	  simp only [CommRingCat.comp_apply] at hnat
650	  rw [hnat]
651	  congr 1
652	  exact (congrArg (fun (m : _ ⟶ _) => (ConcreteCategory.hom m) a)
653	    (Proj.awayMap_awayToSection (𝒜 := homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) hg hx)).symm
654	
655	/-- The transition unit `Xᵢ/Xⱼ` as a section of the overlap, unfolded through the
656	overlap immersion and the away-to-section identification. -/
657	lemma overlapUnit_val_eq (i j : n) :
658	    (overlapUnit n i j).val
659	      = (overlapHom n i j).appTop
660	          ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).topIso.inv
661	            (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n (ULift.{u} ℤ)) (X i * X j)
662	              (awayFraction n i j))) := rfl
663	
664	section Universe0
665	
666	variable (n₀ : Type)
667	
668	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
669	/-- **Base cocycle of the transported overlap units** (`m = 1`): in
670	`Γ(V(i,j,k), O)`, the images of the three transition units `Xᵢ/Xⱼ`, `Xⱼ/Xₖ`,
671	`Xᵢ/Xₖ` under the base-change comorphisms to the common triple overlap satisfy
672	`p_IJ^♯(Xᵢ/Xⱼ) · p_JK^♯(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = p_IK^♯(Xᵢ/Xₖ)`.  This is the geometric heart of
673	the Serre-twist cocycle: it factors all three units through a common map
674	`V(i,j,k) ⟶ D₊(XᵢXⱼXₖ)` and reduces to the fraction identity
675	`(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)(Xⱼ/Xₖ) = Xᵢ/Xₖ` in `A⁰_{XᵢXⱼXₖ}` via `Proj.awayMap_awayToSection`. -/
676	lemma overlapUnit_cocycle_transport (i j k : n₀) :
677	    (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
678	        (overlapUnit n₀ i j).val *
679	      (Scheme.Hom.appTop ((glueData n₀).t' i j k ≫
680	          pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f j k) ((glueData n₀).f j i)))
681	        (overlapUnit n₀ j k).val
682	    = (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.snd ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
683	        (overlapUnit n₀ i k).val := by
684	  have le_ij : Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j * X k)
685	      ≤ Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j) := by
686	    rw [Proj.basicOpen_mul]; exact inf_le_left
687	  have le_jk : Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j * X k)
688	      ≤ Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X j * X k) := by
689	    rw [show (X i * X j * X k : MvPolynomial n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) = (X j * X k) * X i from
690	      by rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm], Proj.basicOpen_mul]; exact inf_le_left
691	  have le_ik : Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j * X k)
692	      ≤ Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X k) := by
693	    rw [show (X i * X j * X k : MvPolynomial n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) = (X i * X k) * X j from
694	      by rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm (X j) (X k), ← mul_assoc], Proj.basicOpen_mul]; exact inf_le_left
695	  have hIJ : (Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k)))
696	        (overlapUnit n₀ i j).val
697	      = (Scheme.Hom.appTop (commonHom n₀ i j k))
698	          ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j * X k)).topIso.inv
699	            (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j * X k)
700	              (awayMap (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X_mem_deg_one n₀ k)
701	                (show X i * X j * X k = (X i * X j) * X k from rfl) (awayFraction n₀ i j)))) := by
702	    rw [overlapUnit_val_eq]
703	    have hmor : Scheme.Hom.appTop (overlapHom n₀ i j) ≫
704	          Scheme.Hom.appTop (pullback.fst ((glueData n₀).f i j) ((glueData n₀).f i k))
705	        = Scheme.Hom.appTop ((Proj (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ))).homOfLE le_ij) ≫
706	          Scheme.Hom.appTop (commonHom n₀ i j k) := by
707	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
708	      exact congrArg Scheme.Hom.appTop (fact_ij n₀ i j k le_ij)
709	    refine (congrArg (fun (m : _ ⟶ _) => (ConcreteCategory.hom m)
710	        ((Proj.basicOpen (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j)).topIso.inv
711	          (Proj.awayToSection (homogeneousSubmodule n₀ (ULift.{0} ℤ)) (X i * X j)
712	            (awayFraction n₀ i j)))) hmor).trans ?_
713	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
714	    exact congrArg (fun z => (ConcreteCategory.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop (commonHom n₀ i j k))) z)
715	      (section_restrict n₀ (X i * X j) (X k) (X_mem_deg_one n₀ k) (X i * X j * X k)
716	        (show X i * X j * X k = (X i * X j) * X k from rfl) le_ij (awayFraction n₀ i j))

10	/-!
11	# Projective morphisms carrying a very ample line bundle
12	
13	Mathlib v4.31 has no projective-morphism class and no (very) ampleness
14	vocabulary.  Following the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118` (comment
15	`C-0002`), this file defines:
16	
17	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf n₀ S m` — the Serre twist `O(m)` on the
18	  relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`, the pullback of
19	  `ProjTwist.serreTwist` from the integral model along `toProjInt`;
20	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange` — `O(m)` commutes with base
21	  change of the ambient projective space;
22	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith π L` — the **projective-with-`L`** predicate:
23	  `π : X ⟶ S` factors through a closed immersion `i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)`
24	  over `S` with `L ≅ i^* O(1)`;
25	
26	with the stability facts the Quot-scheme endgame consumes:
27	
28	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.isProper` — projective morphisms are proper;
29	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.comp_isClosedImmersion` — a closed immersion
30	  into a projective scheme is projective (carrying the restricted bundle);
31	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` — stability under base change
32	  (carrying the pulled-back bundle).
33	
34	Everything is at `Scheme.{0}`: the Serre twist rests on the descent engine
35	`Scheme.Modules.glue`, which is universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`
36	(`GlueDescent.lean:934`).
37	
38	Blueprint: `def:twisting_sheaf`, `def:projective_with`,
39	`lem:projective_with_proper`, `lem:projective_with_closed_immersion`,
40	`lem:projective_with_base_change`
41	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
42	-/
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
45	
46	noncomputable section
47	
48	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
49	
50	namespace ProjectiveSpace
51	
52	variable (n₀ : Type) (S : Scheme.{0})
53	
54	/-- The Serre twist `O(m)` on the relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`:
55	the pullback of the glued twisting sheaf on the integral model. -/
56	def twistingSheaf (m : ℕ) : (ℙ(n₀; S)).Modules :=
57	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (toProjInt n₀ S)).obj (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
58	
59	variable {S} {S' : Scheme.{0}}
60	
61	/-- The Serre twist commutes with base change of the ambient projective
62	space: `(map g)^* O(m) ≅ O(m)`. -/
63	def twistingSheafBaseChange (g : S' ⟶ S) (m : ℕ) :
64	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (map n₀ g)).obj (twistingSheaf n₀ S m) ≅
65	      twistingSheaf n₀ S' m :=
66	  Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (map_toProjInt n₀ g) (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
67	
68	end ProjectiveSpace
69	
70	/-- `π : X ⟶ S` is a **projective morphism carrying the line bundle `L`**
71	([Nitsure] §5, [EGA II] 5.5): there are a closed immersion
72	`i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)` over `S` and an isomorphism of `L` with the
73	pullback of the Serre twist `O(1)`.  This is the faithful encoding of
74	"projective with relatively very ample `L`" settled in inbox `I-0118`. -/
75	def Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith {X S : Scheme.{0}} (π : X ⟶ S) (L : X.Modules) :
76	    Prop :=
77	  ∃ (d : ℕ) (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)),
78	    IsClosedImmersion i ∧ i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π ∧
79	      Nonempty (L ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback i).obj
80	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1))
81	
82	namespace Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
83	
84	variable {X S : Scheme.{0}} {π : X ⟶ S} {L : X.Modules}
85	
86	/-- **Projective morphisms are proper**: a closed immersion is proper, the
87	structural morphism of projective space is proper, and properness is stable
88	under composition. -/
89	theorem isProper (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : IsProper π := by
90	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, -⟩ := h
91	  haveI := hi
92	  rw [← hcomp]
93	  infer_instance
94	
95	/-- **Projective morphisms are locally of finite type**: immediate from
96	`isProper`, since properness extends `LocallyOfFiniteType`.  This lets the
97	Quot-scheme endgame and the Hilbert-polynomial existence theorem derive finite
98	type from projectivity instead of carrying it as a separate hypothesis. -/
99	theorem locallyOfFiniteType (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : LocallyOfFiniteType π :=
100	  haveI := h.isProper; inferInstance
101	
102	/-- **Projective morphisms are separated** (properness extends `IsSeparated`). -/
103	theorem isSeparated (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : IsSeparated π :=
104	  haveI := h.isProper; inferInstance
105	
106	/-- **Projective morphisms are universally closed** (properness extends
107	`UniversallyClosed`). -/
108	theorem universallyClosed (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : UniversallyClosed π :=
109	  haveI := h.isProper; inferInstance
110	
111	/-- **Transfer along an isomorphism of the carried bundle**: `IsProjectiveWith`
112	depends on `L` only up to isomorphism (it records the comparison `L ≅ i^* O(1)`
113	through `Nonempty`), so if `π` is projective carrying `L` and `L ≅ L'`, then `π`
114	is projective carrying `L'`.  Useful for consumers that only have the bundle up
115	to isomorphism. -/
116	theorem of_iso (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {L' : X.Modules} (e : L ≅ L') :
117	    π.IsProjectiveWith L' := by
118	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨eL⟩⟩ := h
119	  exact ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e.symm ≪≫ eL⟩⟩
120	
121	/-- **Composition with a closed immersion**: if `π` is projective carrying
122	`L` and `j` is a closed immersion into `X`, then `j ≫ π` is projective
123	carrying `j^* L`. -/
124	theorem comp_isClosedImmersion (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {Y : Scheme.{0}}
125	    (j : Y ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion j] :
126	    (j ≫ π).IsProjectiveWith ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj L) := by
127	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
128	  haveI := hi
129	  refine ⟨d, j ≫ i, inferInstance, by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp], ?_⟩
130	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫
131	    Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (rfl : j ≫ i = j ≫ i)
132	      (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)⟩
133	
134	/-- The comparison morphism from the base-changed total space into the
135	base-changed projective space. -/
136	private def baseChangeLift {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
137	    (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)) (hcomp : i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π) :
138	    pullback π g ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') :=
139	  (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).lift
140	    (pullback.fst π g ≫ i) (pullback.snd π g)
141	    (by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp, pullback.condition])
142	
143	/-- **Stability under base change**: if `π : X ⟶ S` is projective carrying
144	`L` and `g : S' ⟶ S`, then the base change `X ×_S S' ⟶ S'` is projective
145	carrying the pullback of `L`. -/
146	theorem baseChange (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) :
147	    (pullback.snd π g).IsProjectiveWith
148	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).obj L) := by
149	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
150	  haveI := hi
151	  refine ⟨d, baseChangeLift g i hcomp, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
152	  · -- the comparison square exhibits the lift as the base change of `i`
153	    have h1 : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S')
154	        = pullback.snd π g := IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
155	    have hsq : IsPullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp) (pullback.fst π g)
156	        (ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g) i := by
157	      have hbig : IsPullback
158	          (baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S'))
159	          (pullback.fst π g) g (i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S)) := by
160	        rw [h1, hcomp]
161	        exact (IsPullback.of_hasPullback π g).flip
162	      exact IsPullback.of_right hbig
163	        (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _)
164	        (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).flip
165	    exact MorphismProperty.of_isPullback hsq.flip hi
166	  · exact IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
167	  · refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).mapIso e ≪≫
168	      Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _ :
169	        baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
170	          = pullback.fst π g ≫ i).symm
171	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1) ≪≫ ?_⟩
172	    -- collapse `(lift ≫ map)^* O(1)` to `lift^* (map^* O(1))`, then use the
173	    -- base-change isomorphism of the twist
174	    exact (Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso
175	        (rfl : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
176	          = _) (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)).symm ≪≫
177	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp)).mapIso
178	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange (Fin (d + 1)) g 1)
179	
180	end Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
181	
182	namespace ProjectiveSpace
183	
184	/-- **The structural morphism of relative projective space is itself
185	projective**, carrying the Serre twist `O(1)`: the canonical inhabitant of
186	`IsProjectiveWith`, with the identity closed immersion.  This exhibits
187	`ℙ(Fin (d+1); S) ↘ S` as the universal projective morphism and shows the
188	predicate is non-vacuous. -/
189	theorem isProjectiveWith_over (d : ℕ) (S : Scheme.{0}) :
190	    (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S).IsProjectiveWith (twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1) :=
191	  ⟨d, 𝟙 _, inferInstance, Category.id_comp _,
192	    ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).app _).symm⟩⟩
193	
194	end ProjectiveSpace

196	end AlgebraicGeometry
